rationale.completeness: The core features (field_path, example, expected_type, suggested_field) are implemented, but the exception handlers don't pass valid_fields or field_schemas to enhance_errors(), limiting the typo detection and schema-based example generation in practice.
rationale.correctness: The utility functions are correct, but the integration in exception_handlers.py doesn't fully wire up the enhancement - it calls enhance_errors(errors) without passing the model schema context needed for full functionality.
rationale.style: Well-documented with clear docstrings, follows Python conventions, proper type hints, and good modular structure. The error_enhancement module is cleanly separated.
rationale.quality: Clean, readable code with good abstraction. Comprehensive test coverage including performance tests. Minor gap: T020 (mypy strict type checking) is marked incomplete in tasks.md.
